selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":19,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Los Angeles","employment_status":"full-time student","education_level":"bachelor's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"bachelor's degree"},"current_marital_status":"single","children":0,"migration_status":"immigrant","psychological_feature":"with good mental health","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly one hour daily on social media","hobbies":["football (calcio)","cinema","knitting"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":99,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":69,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":42,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":15,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":23,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "It's important to be cautious when consuming information on social media, as much of it may be fake or misleading. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 2) Use fact-checking websites and tools to validate the accuracy of the information. 3) Be skeptical of sensational or unverifiable claims, especially if they are being pushed by influencers or celebrities. 4) Set your social media platforms to notify you when someone tries to share fake content with you. 5) Use privacy settings to control who can share content with you and avoid seeing misinformation. 6) Follow reputable news sources and fact-checking organizations on social media to stay informed and avoid being misled by fake content."
, "reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be critical of the information shared on these platforms. By verifying the source of the information, using fact-checking websites and tools, being skeptical of sensational or unverifiable claims, setting your social media platforms to notify you when someone tries to share fake content with you, using privacy settings to control who can share content with you and avoid seeing misinformation, and following reputable news sources and fact-checking organizations on social media, you can protect yourself from being misled by fake content."
, "tone": "educated",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: It's important to be cautious when consuming information on social media, as much of it may be fake or misleading. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 2) Use fact-checking websites and tools to validate the accuracy of the information. 3) Be skeptical of sensational or unverifiable claims, especially if they are being pushed by influencers or celebrities. 4) Set your social media platforms to notify you when someone tries to share fake content with you. 5) Use privacy settings to control who can share content with you and avoid seeing misinformation. 6) Follow reputable news sources and fact-checking organizations on social media to stay informed and avoid being misled by fake content.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be critical of the information shared on these platforms. By verifying the source of the information, using fact-checking websites and tools, being skeptical of sensational or unverifiable claims, setting your social media platforms to notify you when someone tries to share fake content with you, using privacy settings to control who can share content with you and avoid seeing misinformation, and following reputable news sources and fact-checking organizations on social media, you can protect yourself from being misled by fake content.
